title: Cystopus clarkei
uses: Cystopus clarkei, also known as the Himalayan Marshwort, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. Its leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. Further studies are required to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications.

parts: Cystopus clarkei is a species of flowering plant, and while there may not be a wealth of information available on its medicinal properties, research suggests that certain parts of the plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine.
